title: betula nana subsp. relicta
intro: Betula nana subsp. relicta, also known as the Arctic dwarf birch, is a subspecies of the birch tree that is native to the Arctic regions. It is a shrub-like plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Betula nana subsp. relicta include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially treating various infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its effectiveness and potential side effects.
